today.headline: Registrar check-in para liberar recomendacao
today.why: Falta o check-in manual do treino; registre para liberar a recomendacao.

mission.name: Meia Forte Janeiro 2027
mission.primary_objective: Correr uma meia maratona forte sem elevar risco de lesão desnecessariamente.
mission.short_term_focus: Maximizar 10K com pacing conservador, recuperação e decisões auditáveis.
mission.interface_role: Interface principal de leitura gerada a partir dos dados versionados do repositório.

current_training_state.coaching_bias: evitar máxima velocidade frequente; proteger Aquiles primeiro

bruna.strategic_limit: progressão segura de 5K/10K para meia forte

latest_shared_workout.display_context: Pace compartilhado informa Bruna apenas com contexto manual/PSE.

latest_matheus_solo.display_context: Esforço Garmin apenas do Matheus; não usar como evolução da Bruna.

current_state.summary_markdown:
# Running Coach State

## Last Update

- Mode: after-workout
- Latest Garmin activity: 2026-05-28 garmin-20260528T171417-1p33km-349s-245ee321 1.33km @4:22
- Latest shared coaching evidence: 2026-05-28 garmin-20260528T161736-7p47km-3039s-17b463bc 7.47km @6:47
- Activities processed: 20
- Workouts modeled: 20
- Missing check-in evidence: 19
- Approved science refs available: 10

## Current Phase

- Phase: ten_k_polish
- Week number: 1
- Weekly rhythm: run Tuesday, Thursday, Sunday; strength Monday/Friday and sometimes Saturday; volleyball Wednesday.

## Current Paces

- Bruna easy/long run estimate: 6:40-7:00/km.
- Bruna strong sustainable estimate: 6:10-6:20/km.
- Bruna threshold estimate: around 6:00/km when recovered.
- Bruna short all-out ceiling from recent rustic race: around 5:50/km; not continuous training pace.
- Matheus recent solo residual speed: 1.33 km at 4:22/km; not used as Bruna evolution evidence.

## Current Risks

- Missing check-ins: 19.
- Matheus Achilles remains the strategic limiter.
- Volleyball counts as neuromuscular load before Thursday sessions.
- Poor sleep, high PSE, strong symptoms, or heavy legs reduce the next workout.

## Next Milestones

- 2026-05-31: diagnostic_race_10k (ten_k_polish) -> planned
- 2026-06-02: easy_run (post_ten_k_recovery) -> planned
- 2026-06-04: easy_run (base) -> planned

## Active Decisions

- Do not compensate missed workouts with volume.
- Keep long runs easy.
- Treat Matheus Garmin physiology as Matheus-only.
- Use shared pace for Bruna only when check-in confirms shared run and Bruna presence.

risk_drivers.label: Vôlei no dia anterior aumenta carga neuromuscular; evitar trabalho máximo.

recent_workouts.decision_after_workout: Smoke operacional; nao usar como decisao de treino.

week_narrative: Semana: 4 corrida(s), 15.0 km no total, sem sessão de qualidade, 1 com a Bruna, consistência boa.

readiness.message: Prontidão parcial: mantenha leve e observe a resposta antes de subir a carga.

goal_feasibility.message: Agressivo mas possível: precisa ~1.8%/mês (limite superior). Exige tudo dando certo — consistência total, talvez um 4º dia, zero lesão. O sistema vai reavaliar a cada prova.

science_refs.title: Prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of the overtraining syndrome: joint consensus statement of the European College of Sport Science and the American College of Sports Medicine
science_refs.authors: ["Romain Meeusen","Martine Duclos","Carl Foster","Andrew Fry","Michael Gleeson","David Nieman","John Raglin","Gerard Rietjens","Juergen Steinacker","Axel Urhausen"]
science_refs.finding: Persistent fatigue, performance decrement, sleep disturbance, mood change, and illness/injury signs require conservative management and exclusion of medical causes.
science_refs.practical_application: Treat red-flag symptom clusters as a reason to stop performance-seeking recommendations and bias toward recovery or medical evaluation.
science_refs.limits: Consensus guidance for overtraining syndrome, not a diagnostic engine for acute injury or illness.

science_refs.title: International Olympic Committee consensus statement on load in sport and risk of injury: how much is too much?
science_refs.authors: ["Torbjoern Soligard","Martin Schwellnus","Juan-M. Alonso","Roald Bahr","Ben Clarsen","H. Paul Dijkstra","Tim Gabbett","Michael Gleeson","Martin Hagglund","Mark R. Hutchinson","Christa Janse van Rensburg","Karim M. Khan","Romain Meeusen","John W. Orchard","Babette M. Pluim","Martin Raftery","Richard Budgett","Lars Engebretsen"]
science_refs.finding: Injury risk is related to both external and internal load, with rapid load changes and inadequate recovery increasing risk.
science_refs.practical_application: Reduce or defer load after unusually hard efforts, high perceived exertion, or stacked stressors instead of adding quality work.
science_refs.limits: Broad multi-sport consensus; individual thresholds require athlete-specific history.

science_refs.title: Continued sports activity, using a pain-monitoring model, during rehabilitation in patients with Achilles tendinopathy: a randomized controlled study
science_refs.authors: ["Karin Graevare Silbernagel","Roland Thomee","Bengt I. Eriksson","Jon Karlsson"]
science_refs.finding: Achilles loading activity during rehab did not show negative effects when governed by a pain-monitoring model.
science_refs.practical_application: Use Achilles symptom severity to gate pacing and quality workouts; controlled loading may continue only inside conservative pain limits.
science_refs.limits: Clinical tendinopathy population; does not authorize ignoring worsening pain, red flags, or professional evaluation.

science_refs.title: Sleep Interventions Designed to Improve Athletic Performance and Recovery: A Systematic Review of Current Approaches
science_refs.finding: Sleep is important for aspects of athletic performance and recovery, although intervention evidence varies by population and protocol.
science_refs.practical_application: Treat poor sleep as a load-management input and reduce the next workout when paired with fatigue or hard prior efforts.
science_refs.limits: Systematic review notes heterogeneous methods and incomplete guidance for all athlete groups.

science_refs.title: Training stress, neuromuscular fatigue and well-being in volleyball: a systematic review
science_refs.authors: ["André Rebelo","João R. Pereira","Paulo Cunha","Manuel J. Coelho-e-Silva","João Valente-dos-Santos"]
science_refs.journal_or_publisher: BMC Sports Science, Medicine and Rehabilitation
science_refs.finding: Volleyball monitoring literature links training stress measures with neuromuscular fatigue and well-being assessments, including daily internal load and jump-based external load monitoring.
science_refs.practical_application: Treat volleyball or jumping exposure as a neuromuscular load signal to consider alongside athlete-reported fatigue, soreness, and readiness; it does not quantify next-day running risk by itself.
science_refs.limits: Systematic review of volleyball monitoring studies; does not quantify next-day running risk or prescribe running-specific recovery thresholds.

science_refs.title: American College of Sports Medicine position stand. Quantity and quality of exercise for developing and maintaining cardiorespiratory, musculoskeletal, and neuromotor fitness in apparently healthy adults
science_refs.authors: ["Carol Ewing Garber","Bryan Blissmer","Michael R. Deschenes","Barry A. Franklin","Michael J. Lamonte","I-Min Lee","David C. Nieman","David P. Swain"]
science_refs.finding: Regular cardiorespiratory and resistance exercise supports maintenance and improvement of physical fitness.
science_refs.practical_application: When guardrails are clear, maintain the planned easy or long aerobic work rather than adding unnecessary complexity.
science_refs.limits: General adult prescription guidance, not runner-specific periodization.

science_refs.title: Quantifying training intensity distribution in elite endurance athletes: is there evidence for an optimal distribution?
science_refs.journal_or_publisher: Scandinavian Journal of Medicine & Science in Sports
science_refs.finding: Observed endurance training was organized with most sessions below the first ventilatory threshold and a smaller share of high-intensity work.
science_refs.practical_application: Keep most weekly running easy and avoid turning easy or long runs into threshold work.
science_refs.limits: Descriptive evidence from elite skiers; not a universal prescription for recreational runners.

science_refs.title: Effect of training on lactate/ventilatory thresholds: a meta-analysis
science_refs.finding: Training near lactate or ventilatory threshold can improve thresholds, with conditioned athletes often needing higher intensities.
science_refs.practical_application: Use threshold work as a bounded quality stimulus, and defer it when recovery or injury guardrails are active.
science_refs.limits: Meta-analysis spans mixed populations and protocols; pace prescriptions still need individual calibration.

science_refs.title: Effects of Strength Training on Running Economy in Highly Trained Runners: A Systematic Review With Meta-Analysis of Controlled Trials
science_refs.journal_or_publisher: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research
science_refs.finding: Strength training programs can improve running economy in trained runners.
science_refs.practical_application: Include strength as supportive training, while avoiding heavy strength work when recovery flags suggest load reduction.
science_refs.limits: Focused on trained runners and controlled trials; transfer depends on exercise selection and recovery management.

science_refs.title: Athletic Records and Human Endurance
science_refs.finding: Race time scales with distance by t2 = t1 * (d2/d1)^1.06, enabling cross-distance performance prediction.
science_refs.practical_application: Project half-marathon pace and training zones from a recent shorter race; a conservative exponent (1.08) suits recreational runners.
science_refs.limits: An empirical model; individual endurance profiles and training specificity shift the true exponent.

llm_context.data_contract: Garmin physiology is Matheus-only. Shared pace/distance/time can be used for Bruna only when shared_run=true and bruna_present=true.
forbidden_claims: Do not use Matheus solo pace as Bruna evolution
Do not infer Bruna heart rate from Garmin heart rate
Do not prescribe all-out speed when evidence is missing
Do not cite science outside approved_science_refs
Do not diagnose injury or medical conditions

latest_llm_recommendation.summary: Treino compartilhado de 7,47km concluído com PSE 7 e sono regular, dia após vôlei. Guardrail confirma redução de carga para a corrida diagnóstica de 10k em 31/05. Bruna deve correr de forma controlada sem tentar bater teto de velocidade. Matheus condiciona participação ao Aquiles em 0 na manhã do evento.
latest_llm_recommendation.what_workout_showed: O treino compartilhado de 2026-05-28 (7,47km a 6:47/km, PSE 7, vôlei no dia anterior, sono regular) confirma que ambos completaram volume operacional dentro da faixa de ritmo fácil-moderado de Bruna (6:40-7:00/km). A PSE 7 com sono regular e carga de vôlei prévia indica que o esforço percebido foi elevado para o ritmo executado, sinalizando fadiga neuromuscular residual. O segmento solo de Matheus (1,33km a 4:22/km) é dado exclusivo de fisiologia Matheus e não é utilizado como evidência de evolução de Bruna. Ausência de HR de Bruna impede validação da zona de intensidade cardiovascular.
latest_llm_recommendation.risk_assessment: Risco moderado. Volleyball na quarta-feira (dia anterior ao treino de quinta) gerou carga neuromuscular adicional confirmada pelo check-in. O treino compartilhado de 7,47km a 6:47/km com PSE 7 e sono regular indica fadiga residual acumulada. Ausência de dados de frequência cardíaca de Bruna impede avaliação precisa da carga cardiovascular real. Aquiles de Matheus em 0 é positivo, mas permanece como limitador estratégico para o dia da corrida. Sem check-in de sono e PSE para 2026-05-31, a confiança na prescrição de intensidade máxima é reduzida. Guardrail determinístico confirma redução como ação prioritária.
latest_llm_recommendation.next_workout: Corrida diagnóstica 10k em 2026-05-31. Bruna deve iniciar em ritmo conservador de 6:20-6:30/km nos primeiros 3km, mantendo percepção de esforço controlada (PSE alvo 7-8). Não tentar superar o teto de 5:50/km registrado na corrida rústica. Matheus deve gerenciar o esforço respeitando o tendão de Aquiles; ritmo de corrida compartilhada somente se Aquiles estiver em 0 na manhã do evento. Caso Aquiles apresente qualquer desconforto matinal, aplicar decisão bruna_without_matheus. Não compensar volume ou intensidade em função dos treinos perdidos anteriores.
latest_llm_recommendation.stale_message: Análise da IA desatualizada para o último treino. A decisão atual está em 'O que fazer hoje'. Rode a análise para atualizar.

hard_rules: Se Bruna PSE >= 9 em treino, próxima sessão deve ser leve ou off.
Se Bruna tiver sintomas fortes, reduzir intensidade.
Se Aquiles do Matheus > 3/10, remover velocidade, subidas e descidas.
Se teve vôlei no dia anterior, evitar trabalho máximo.
Nunca compensar treino perdido com volume extra.

presentation_warnings: Pace solo do Matheus não pode ser renderizado como evolução da Bruna.
FC, cadência, potência, contato com solo e passada do Garmin são apenas do Matheus.
Zonas da Bruna são faixas conservadoras até aumentar evidência manual de FC/PSE.
Treino perdido não deve ser compensado com volume extra.
